title: ⭐⭐ Review: Used to vs Would vs Be/Get Used to

questions.prompt: Complete: "My brother ___ be very shy, but now he's very confident."
questions.explanation: "be" is a state verb, so only "used to" can express this past state: My brother used to be shy. "would" is only for repeated actions, not states, "is used to" means accustomed to, and "was getting used to" means becoming accustomed.

questions.prompt: Complete: "On weekends, we ___ go fishing at the lake with our father."
questions.explanation: Going fishing is a repeated past action with a time frame ("on weekends"), so "would" fits the narrative pattern: we would go fishing. "use to" is incorrect — the past form is "used to" (with -d). "are/got used to" mean accustomed to, a different meaning.

questions.prompt: Find the ERROR: "They would know all their neighbours when they lived in the village."
questions.explanation: The error is "would know": know is a state verb, so "would" cannot replace "used to" here. The correct sentence is: They used to know all their neighbours. "would" is only valid with action verbs, never with state verbs like know.

questions.prompt: Complete: "She didn't ___ eating spicy food, but she loves it now."
questions.explanation: In the negative form with "didn't", the -d disappears: she didn't use to eat spicy food. "didn't used to" keeps the -d wrongly after did. "would" doesn't form negatives the same way in this context, and "get used to" means to become accustomed.

questions.prompt: Complete: "My grandfather ___ tell us old stories about the war after dinner."
questions.explanation: Telling stories is a repeated past action (action verb "tell"), so "would" is the perfect nostalgic narrative choice: my grandfather would tell us stories. "is/was/gets used to" all mean accustomed to and take -ing after them.

questions.prompt: Complete: "He has just moved to the city and is slowly ___ the noise."
questions.explanation: "get used to + -ing/noun" describes the process of becoming accustomed: he is getting used to the noise. "used to" (without be/get) is the past habit structure, "would" describes repeated past actions, and "use to" is only correct after did in a question or negative.
